mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding economic activity that lifts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward 20-day forward returns following positive PAYEMS momentum are non-positive in more than half of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate above 4.5 percent signals restrictive monetary policy that raises equity discount rates and depresses S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5 percent over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A restrictive federal funds rate combined with a rising unemployment rate tightens financial conditions and weakens consumer demand simultaneously, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.0% and UNRATE increases over prior 3 months
failure_condition: The joint signal does not produce negative 60-day returns in at least 60 percent of triggered quarters during walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment during post-shock recovery signals accelerating wage-driven consumption growth that boosts corporate revenues and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next sixty trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns following month-over-month UNRATE declines are not positive in more than 55 percent of instances during post-shock recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in retail sales signals robust consumer demand that lifts revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive RSAFS surprises are not positive in more than 55 percent of months over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production serves as a coincident indicator of manufacturing profitability and capacity utilization, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following positive INDPRO trends do not outperform zero in more than 55 percent of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts below 1400 thousand units signal weakening construction activity and downstream durable goods demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in fewer than 55 percent of triggered months when HOUST is below 1400 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index growth exceeding 0.3 percent month-over-month signals monetary policy persistence that compresses valuation multiples and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next twenty trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55 percent of months when CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3 percent month-over-month over any 2-year rolling window.
